# Execution Logs

**Slug:** `execution-logs` ┬Ę **Version:** `2021-03` ┬Ę **Tools:** 4

Retrieve logs about task runs.

## untagged

### `execution_logs__delete_monitoring_executions_id_logs`

```
DELETE /monitoring/executions/{id}/logs
```

Delete logs of a specified execution

This endpoint allows users with the `Execution Logs - Delete` permission  (ID: `EXECUTION_LOGS_DELETE`) to delete logs for a given execution identified by its ID. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | path | `string` | yes |  |


### `execution_logs__get_monitoring_executions_id_logs`

```
GET /monitoring/executions/{id}/logs
```

Get task execution logs

Get run logs of a given task by its execution ID (also referred to as run ID)

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | path | `string` | yes |  |
| `count` | query | `number` | no | Number of log entries to return. The default value is 50 and the maximum value is 200 |
| `endTime` | query | `string` | no | latest date and time of the logs to be retrieved. By default, current time is used |
| `order` | query | `string` | no | The way in which the returned logs are sorted by their timestamps. The default value is DESC, meaning the latest logs are put first |
| `startIndex` | query | `number` | no | Start position of the pagination of the logs, greater or equal to 0 |
| `startTime` | query | `string` | no | Earliest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, logs are retrieved from the start time of the run |


### `execution_logs__post_monitoring_executions_id_logs`

```
POST /monitoring/executions/{id}/logs
```

Generate logs for a completed or ongoing execution

This endpoint generates the log of a given task run and returns a download token for this log (maximum log size: 100,000).

This token is valid for one hour.    

If the desired log size exceeds the 100,000-entry limit, use the time range parameters (`startTime` or `endTime` or both) to split the requests. Adjust the time range for each request to retrieve all log entries by making multiple calls.    
For example, use `endTime` in each request to retrieve up to 100,000 log entries before that time. Repeat with earlier `endTime` values until you have retrieved the desired log history.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | path | `string` | yes |  |
| `fileFormat` | query | `"JSON" | "TEXT"` | no | Format of the log file to be generated. The available formats are `json` and `text`. |
| `endTime` | query | `string` | no | Latest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, current time is used |
| `newFileName` | query | `boolean` | no | This parameter indicates whether you need to include task IDs and execution IDs in the filename of the log to be generated. It generates a distinctive file name reading like: `taskID_executionID.log`  |
| `startTime` | query | `string` | no | Earliest date and time of the logs to be put in the generated log. By default, logs are retrieved from the start time of the run |


### `execution_logs__post_monitoring_executions_id_logs_status`

```
POST /monitoring/executions/{id}/logs/status
```

Check log generation status and return download URL

Check the status of the generated log and return a download URL.

You need to put the download token in the body.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | path | `string` | yes |  |

**Request body** (`application/json`): `string`.
